gpu_suitability.reasoning: Sparse matrix-vector operations are well-suited for GPU acceleration due to high parallelism. The SYMGS algorithm has data dependencies (Gauss-Seidel requires sequential updates), but parallel variants exist (hybrid Gauss-Seidel, block Jacobi preconditioning).
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Use hybrid approach: 1) Implement parallel Gauss-Seidel variant (coloring) for GPU, 2) Use optimized sparse matrix formats (CSR, ELL, HYB) from cuSPARSE, 3) Overlap CPU-GPU transfers with computation, 4) Consider mixed-precision arithmetic for memory-bound operations.
